file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - "master"
  pull_request:

env:
  GOLANGCILINT_VERSION: "2.11.4"

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: "1.26.3"
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v9
        with:
          version: v${{ env.GOLANGCILINT_VERSION }}

  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            test_target: test-race
            GOARCH: amd64
            # race requires cgo, otherwise should not be needed
            CGO_ENABLED: 1
          # build and test on 32 bit, does not support race
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            test_target: test
            GOARCH: "386"
            CGO_ENABLED: 0
          - os: macos-latest
            test_target: test
            GOARCH: amd64
            CGO_ENABLED: 0
          - os: windows-latest
            test_target: test
            GOARCH: amd64
            CGO_ENABLED: 0
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      # only run cli binary tests on linux
      - name: Install expect
        if: ${{ matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest' }}
        run: sudo apt-get install expect
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v3
        with:
          go-version: "1.26.3"
      - name: Test
        env:
          CGO_ENABLED: ${{ matrix.CGO_ENABLED }}
          GOARCH: ${{ matrix.GOARCH }}
        run: make ${{ matrix.test_target }}

content.thinking:
Perfect! Now I understand what the test job does. Looking at the job name "test (ubuntu-latest, test, 386, 0)", this corresponds to the second matrix element:
- os: ubuntu-latest
- test_target: test
- GOARCH: "386"
- CGO_ENABLED: 0

The job:
1. Installs expect on ubuntu-latest
2. Checks out the code (using actions/checkout@v3)
3. Sets up Go 1.26.3 (using actions/setup-go@v3)
4. Sets env vars: CGO_ENABLED=0, GOARCH=386
5. Runs: make test

content.content:
1	GO_BUILD_FLAGS ?= -trimpath
2	GO_BUILD_LDFLAGS ?= -s -w
3	
4	all: test fq
5	
6	# used to force make to always redo
7	.PHONY: always
8	
9	fq: always
10		CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o fq -ldflags "${GO_BUILD_LDFLAGS}" ${GO_BUILD_FLAGS} .
11	
12	test: always testgo testjq testcli
13	test-race: always testgo-race testjq testcli
14	
15	# figure out all go packages with test files
16	testgo: PKGS=$(shell find . -name "*_test.go" | xargs -n 1 dirname | sort | uniq)
17	testgo: always
18		go test -timeout 20m ${RACE} ${VERBOSE} ${COVER} ${PKGS}
19	
20	testgo-race: RACE=-race
21	testgo-race: testgo
22	
23	testjq: $(shell find . -name "*.jq.test")
24	%.jq.test: fq
25		@echo $@
26	# set HOME to something non-existing to not accidentally include from config dir
27		@HOME=/does_not_exist ./fq -rRs -L pkg/interp 'include "jqtest"; run_tests' $@
28	
29	testcli: fq
30		@pkg/cli/test_exp.sh ./fq pkg/cli/test_repl.exp
31		@pkg/cli/test_exp.sh ./fq pkg/cli/test_cli_ctrlc.exp
32		@pkg/cli/test_exp.sh ./fq pkg/cli/test_cli_ctrld.exp
33	
34	cover: COVER=-cover -coverpkg=./... -coverprofile=cover.out
35	cover: test
36		go tool cover -html=cover.out -o cover.out.html
37		cat cover.out.html | grep '<option value="file' | sed -E 's/.*>(.*) \((.*)%\)<.*/\2 \1/' | sort -rn
38	
39	doc: always
40	doc: $(wildcard doc/*.svg.sh)
41	doc: $(wildcard *.md doc/*.md)
42	
43	%.md: fq
44		@doc/mdsh.sh ./fq $@
45	
46	doc/%.svg.sh: fq
47		(cd doc ; ../$@ ../fq) | go run github.com/wader/ansisvg@master > $(@:.svg.sh=.svg)
48	
49	doc/formats.svg: fq
50		@# ignore graphviz version as it causes diff when nothing has changed
51		./fq -rnf doc/formats_diagram.jq | dot -Tsvg | sed 's/Generated by graphviz.*//' >doc/formats.svg
52	
53	doc/file.mp3: Makefile
54		ffmpeg -y -f lavfi -i sine -f lavfi -i testsrc -map 0:0 -map 1:0 -t 20ms "$@"
55	
56	doc/file.mp4: Makefile
57		ffmpeg -y -f lavfi -i sine -f lavfi -i testsrc -c:a aac -c:v h264 -f mp4 -t 20ms "$@"
58	
59	gogenerate: always
60		go generate -x ./...
61	
62	lint: always
63	# bump: make-golangci-lint /golangci-lint@v([\d.]+)/ git:https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint.git|^2
64	# bump: make-golangci-lint link "Release notes" https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/releases/tag/v$LATEST
65		go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@v2.11.4 run
66	
67	depgraph.svg: always
68		go run github.com/kisielk/godepgraph@latest github.com/wader/fq | dot -Tsvg -o godepgraph.svg
69	
70	# make memprof ARGS=". test.mp3"
71	# make cpuprof ARGS=". test.mp3"
72	prof: always
73		go build -tags profile -o fq.prof .
74		CPUPROFILE=fq.cpu.prof MEMPROFILE=fq.mem.prof ./fq.prof ${ARGS}
75	memprof: prof
76		go tool pprof -http :5555 fq.prof fq.mem.prof
77	cpuprof: prof
78		go tool pprof -http :5555 fq.prof fq.cpu.prof
79	
80	update-gomod: always
81		GOPROXY=direct go get github.com/wader/gojq@fq
82		go mod tidy
83	
84	# Usage: make fuzz # fuzz all formats
85	# Usage: make fuzz GROUP=mp4 # fuzz a group (each format is a group also)
86	# TODO: as decode recovers panic and "repanics" unrecoverable errors this is a bit hacky at the moment
87	# Retrigger:
88	# try to decode crash with all formats in order to see which one panicked:
89	# cat format/testdata/fuzz/FuzzFormats/... | go run dev/fuzzbytes.go | go run . -d bytes '. as $b | formats | keys[] as $f | $b | decode($f)'
90	# convert crash into raw bytes:

92	# fq -n '"..." | from_base64 | ...'
93	fuzz: always

99	release: always

101	release:

content.thinking:
Great! So "make test" runs:
1. testgo: runs go tests
2. testjq: runs jq tests  
3. testcli: runs CLI tests using expect

Now I understand what needs to be done. I need to:
1. Create a Dockerfile that installs:
   - Ubuntu base image
   - Go 1.26.3
   - expect (for CLI tests)
   - Build tools
   
2. Create a run.sh that:
   - Sets CGO_ENABLED=0 and GOARCH=386 environment variables
   - Runs "make test"

Let me check the existing Dockerfile and run.sh files to see what's already there.

content.content:
1	FROM ubuntu:22.04
2	
3	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
4	ENV GOVERSION=1.26.3
5	
6	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
7	    wget \
8	    git \
9	    expect \
10	    build-essential \
11	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
12	
13	RUN wget -q https://go.dev/dl/go${GOVERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
14	    tar -C /usr/local -xzf go${GOVERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
15	    rm go${GOVERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz
16	
17	ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"
18	
19	WORKDIR /app
20	COPY repo/ /app/
21	
22	RUN go mod download
23	
24	COPY run.sh /run.sh
25	RUN chmod +x /run.sh
26	
27	ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]
28

file.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV GOVERSION=1.26.3

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    git \
    expect \
    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN wget -q https://go.dev/dl/go${GOVERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    tar -C /usr/local -xzf go${GOVERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz && \
    rm go${GOVERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz

ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"

WORKDIR /app
COPY repo/ /app/

RUN go mod download

COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

content.thinking: Good! Now let me check the run.sh file.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -e
3	
4	export CGO_ENABLED=0
5	export GOARCH=386
6	
7	make test
8	
9	FINAL_STATUS=SUCCESS
10	echo "FINAL_STATUS = $FINAL_STATUS"
11

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

export CGO_ENABLED=0
export GOARCH=386

make test

FINAL_STATUS=SUCCESS
echo "FINAL_STATUS = $FINAL_STATUS"

content.thinking:
The files look reasonable! However, there's a potential issue. The problem is that the Go binary being downloaded is for amd64 architecture, but we're trying to test on the 386 (32-bit) architecture. 

For 32-bit testing, we can still use a 64-bit Go compiler and set the GOARCH environment variable to 386 to cross-compile. But there might be issues with dependencies or system requirements.

Let me build and run the image to see what happens.

5. Runs all make test targets as specified in the CI workflow

The job "test (ubuntu-latest, test, 386, 0)" has been successfully reproduced and confirmed working.
